plain_definition: A Financial Action Task Force (FATF) anti-money-laundering standard, adopted into law separately by each jurisdiction, requiring that information about the sender (originator) and recipient (beneficiary) of a funds or crypto-asset transfer 'travel' with that transfer between the financial institutions or crypto-asset/virtual-asset service providers involved, so the transfer can be traced. It is not itself an EU, UK, or Hong Kong law -- FATF is not a regulator any jurisdiction covered by this radar reports to -- but each jurisdiction covered by this radar has implemented its own version. In the EU, Regulation (EU) 2023/1113 (the recast Transfer of Funds Regulation) extended the rule to crypto-asset transfers by crypto-asset service providers from 30 December 2024, with EBA implementing guidelines applying from the same date. In Hong Kong, licensed virtual asset service providers are subject to their own travel-rule requirement under AMLO guidance, with immediate submission of the required information required since 1 January 2024.
formal_definition: In the EU, Regulation (EU) 2023/1113 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 31 May 2023 requires that transfers of funds and crypto-assets be accompanied by 'information on the originators and beneficiaries of those transfers' (recast Transfer of Funds Regulation, applicable from 30 December 2024), implemented for crypto-asset service providers by EBA's Guidelines on information requirements under Regulation (EU) 2023/1113 (published 4 July 2024, applicable from 30 December 2024). The underlying international standard is FATF Recommendation 16, which is not itself a source this radar treats as an official regulator of any covered jurisdiction.
